result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The current reference list covers foundational work on in-the-wild distribution shifts (WILDS) and the theoretical mechanism of shortcut learning. However, it lacks citations for established evaluation methodologies, comparative analyses of training paradigms under environmental stress, and concrete benchmarks for natural corruptions that directly model real-world deployment conditions (e.g., sensor noise, weather, compression artifacts). Adding a foundational robustness benchmark paper will cover multiple citation categories simultaneously: it establishes an evaluation methodology, introduces a widely-used dataset/model benchmark, enables comparison between standard and robust training regimes, highlights gaps in accuracy-centric evaluation, and supports arguments about pre-deployment stress testing. The most critical missing citation is the seminal work on benchmarking neural network robustness to common corruptions, which directly complements the existing literature by shif
